title: Oldenlandia elegans
intro: Oldenlandia elegans, also known as "thorny grass" or "thorny oldenlandia," is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to its health benefits, including wound healing, fever reduction, and immune system enhancement. The bioactive constituents of Oldenlandia elegans include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Oldenlandia elegans is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the form of poultices or ointments to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Oldenlandia elegans is classified as a species within the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee family. This classification places it within the order Gentianales, which includes other flowering plant families such as the Apocynaceae and Loganiaceae. The genus Oldenlandia is a group of flowering plants that are native to various parts of the world, including tropical and subtropical regions. Within the Rubiaceae family, Oldenlandia elegans is a member of a larger clade that includes many other species of flowering plants. The specific classification of Oldenlandia elegans is as follows: family Rubiaceae, genus Oldenlandia, species O. elegans.
